title: /Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/.claude 内容を表示せよ
summary_text: **Findings** 1. High: planning diversity is not explicitly required; current text only enforces parallel lanes. See [CODEX.md](/Users/masayuki/.claude/CODEX.md#L23). 2. High: implementation does not explicitly require active `codex-subagent` usage. See [CODEX.md](/Users/masayuki/.claude/CODEX.md#L21). 3. Medium: `PROJECT-OS` has no field to record planning diversity evidence or subagent evidence, so compliance is hard to audit. See [PROJECT-OS.md](/Users/masayuki/.claude/docs/PROJECT-OS.md#L17). 4. Medium: no explicit `BLOCKED` rule when diversity/subagent requirements cannot be met, which allows silent single-lane fallback risk. See [CODEX.md](/Users/masayuki/.claude/CODEX.md#L25), [PROJECT-OS.md](/Users/masayuki/.claude/docs/PROJECT-OS.md#L33). **Smallest wording changes** 1. Add to Kernel Contract in [CODEX.md](/Users/masayuki/.claude/CODEX.md): `- During planning, Kernel must actively maximize diversity via multi-agent work: run at least three materially distinct planning lanes and synthesize only after comparing divergent proposals.` 2. Add to Kernel Contract in [CODEX.md](/Users/masayuki/.claude/CODEX.md): `- During implementation, Kernel must keep the same multi-agent diversity and actively use Codex subagents for decomposition, execution, and verification lanes; implementation without Codex subagents is non-compliant unless the task is trivial.` 3. Add to Kernel Contract in [CODEX.md](/Users/masayuki/.claude/CODEX.md): `- If required planning diversity or Codex-subagent lanes cannot be established, set status to BLOCKED instead of collapsing to single-lane execution.` 4. Add Ticket fields in [PROJECT-OS.md](/Users/masayuki/.claude/docs/PROJECT-OS.md#L17): `- planning_diversity_evidence` `- implementation_subagents` `- blocked_reason` 5. Add one line to Handoff Protocol in [PROJECT-OS.md](/Users/masayuki/.claude/docs/PROJECT-OS.md#L35): `5. Record lane diversity evidence and Codex-subagent usage evidence before handoff.`
